name: Ronda Rousey

qualityWinLedger.context: Elite bantamweight rival and future UFC champion.
Olympic medalist and top bantamweight title challenger.
Ranked inaugural UFC bantamweight title challenger.
Undefeated title challenger, softer contender context.

primeWindowDetail.endReason: Holm did not end it alone because Rousey returned directly into another title fight. Nunes is endpoint.

lossContextEvents.context: Rousey returned directly into another UFC title fight.

shortCase: Rousey is the original women’s UFC superstar case: historic title dominance, instant finishes, and one of the most important short peaks in company history.
bestArgument: Rousey wins comparisons when historic impact, title-finishing dominance, and short-peak aura outweigh long-term resume depth.
counterArgument: Rousey’s argument is impact and dominance. Nobody on the women’s board changed the UFC faster, but the resume depth is not Nunes or Valentina level.
whyRankedHere: Rousey belongs in this women’s tier because her UFC peak mattered enormously. She was the original women’s UFC champion, defended repeatedly, and finished fights in a way that made her feel almost inevitable during the early run.
whyNotHigher: She does not rank higher because the run was short and the ending was severe. The Holm and Nunes losses damaged the aura, and the later women’s champions built deeper UFC resumes.
finalTakeaway: Rousey is the original women’s UFC force: historically massive, brutally dominant for a short window, and held back by a short run with a rough finish.
